# multiservice — GitOps manifests

Generated by [gitops-scaffold](https://github.com/06ninjatronics/gitops-scaffold)
from `tests/fixtures/compose/multi-service-compose.yml`.

**Confidence: 80%** — review every item under "Needs
review" below before applying these manifests to a cluster.

> This project generates maintainable GitOps scaffolding, not automatically
> production-ready Kubernetes deployments. Every manifest here is meant to
> be readable, idiomatic YAML you'd be comfortable maintaining by hand —
> review it, don't just apply it blind.

## Namespace

`apps`

## Services

### web

- Image: `myorg/web:2.3.1`
- Ports: 8080/TCP (published on host port 8080 in Compose)
- Environment variables:
  - `DATABASE_URL` = `postgres://db:5432/app`
- Port-forward:

  ```sh
  kubectl port-forward -n apps deploy/web 8080:8080
  ```
### db

- Image: `postgres:16.2`
- Ports: none detected
- Environment variables:
  - `POSTGRES_DB` = `app`
- **Secret required** — the Deployment will not become Ready until
  `db-secret` exists in namespace `apps`.
  - Required keys: POSTGRES_PASSWORD

  ```sh
  kubectl create secret generic db-secret -n apps \
    --from-literal=POSTGRES_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME
  ```
- Volumes:
  - `/var/lib/postgresql/data` → PVC `db-data` (1Gi)
### redis

- Image: `redis:7.2`
- Ports: none detected

## Validation

```sh
gitops-scaffold validate .
```

If `kubectl` is installed, `gitops-scaffold validate . --kubectl` additionally
runs `kubectl kustomize .` to confirm this directory builds cleanly.

## Needs review

- [ ] Service 'db' expects a live Secret named 'db-secret' — the Deployment will not become Ready until it exists. Required keys: POSTGRES_PASSWORD. Optional keys: none.
- [ ] Service 'web': runtime user could not be determined; set securityContext explicitly.
- [ ] Service 'web': no health check was detected. Add a readiness/liveness probe manually.
- [ ] Service 'db': runtime user could not be determined; set securityContext explicitly.
- [ ] Service 'db': no health check was detected. Add a readiness/liveness probe manually.
- [ ] Service 'redis': runtime user could not be determined; set securityContext explicitly.
- [ ] Service 'redis': no health check was detected. Add a readiness/liveness probe manually.

## Skipped

- No Service generated for 'db': it exposes no ports.
- No Service generated for 'redis': it exposes no ports.

## Limitations

- Compose's `restart:` policy has no Deployment equivalent — Deployments
  always maintain their desired replica count regardless of it.
- Healthcheck timing fields approximate Compose's semantics but aren't
  identical (see `docs/generation.md`).
- PVC storage sizes and the exact UID/GID a container runs as are never
  guessed — see the review items above.
- `build:`-only services (no `image:`) aren't supported; that service's
  manifests are skipped entirely.

## Next steps

- [ ] Populate the real Secret(s) named above — affected Deployments will
      not become Ready until they exist.
- [ ] Review every `REVIEW REQUIRED` marker in the generated YAML.
- [ ] Add an Ingress if this needs to be reachable from outside the cluster
      (`gitops-scaffold generate ... --ingress-host ... --ingress-class ...
      --tls-secret ... --cluster-issuer ...`) and confirm TLS/DNS.
- [ ] Confirm each container's runtime user is non-root where possible.
- [ ] Test that persistent data actually survives a pod restart before
      relying on any generated PVC in production.
